evidence_quotes.quote: There are thousands of examples of videogames that required online connections that, once the publisher rightfully decided to stop supporting, were rendered completely inaccessible regardless of if you had bought a copy, be it digitally or physically, or not.[1]

Stop Killing Games campaign has shown that there is a genuine movement from the public to require videogame publishers to implement end-of-life plans such as disabling online-only features or allowing the player to host their own server if the game is built around it's online aspect.

paragraphs.text: Adding on the already existing list of practices that the Digital Fairness Act aims to push back against, I'd like to suggest that there's a case to be made about the rightful ownership of videogames and media in general that consumers paid for (be it a one-time purchase or smaller, additional content that was purchased later on) to be included inside the afornmentioned act. With modern day subscription models and videogames that always require you to connect to the publisher's servers in order for you to access the game and it's features, it's extremely important for the consumer to not only be informed of these services shutting down but to continue being able to utilize or play whatever they paid for and own. There are thousands of examples of videogames that required online connections that, once the publisher rightfully decided to stop supporting, were rendered completely inaccessible regardless of if you had bought a copy, be it digitally or physically, or not.[1] The Stop Killing Games campaign has shown that there is a genuine movement from the public to require videogame publishers to implement end-of-life plans such as disabling online-only features or allowing the player to host their own server if the game is built around it's online aspect. I think this has proven that there is a space within EU law for the protection of consumer-bought videogames and media within the wider landscape of digital purchases. [1] Stop Killing Games Wiki's Dead Game List
